projects.title: Quick Sort (In‑Place)
projects.description: Implement the quicksort algorithm to sort an array in ascending order. Choose the last element as pivot and perform in‑place partitioning.

projects.title: Merge Sort
projects.description: Implement merge sort to sort an array. Use an auxiliary array for merging.

projects.title: Heap Sort (using array‑based heap)
projects.description: Implement heap sort by first building a max‑heap from the array, then repeatedly extracting the maximum.
projects.learningOutcome: Heap data structure, heapify, in‑place sorting.

projects.title: Counting Sort (for non‑negative integers)
projects.description: Sort an array of non‑negative integers using counting sort. Assume the range of values is not too large.

projects.title: Radix Sort (using counting sort as subroutine)
projects.description: Implement radix sort for an array of non‑negative integers. Sort by each digit from least significant to most significant.

projects.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
projects.description: Given two sorted arrays, find the median of the combined array in O(log(min(m, n))) time.

projects.title: Kth Smallest Element (Quickselect)
projects.description: Find the kth smallest element in an unsorted array using the quickselect algorithm (average O(n)).
projects.exampleText:
Enter size: 7
Enter elements: 10 4 5 8 6 11 26
Enter k (1‑based): 3

projects.title: Next Permutation
projects.description: Rearrange an array into the lexicographically next greater permutation. If not possible, rearrange into the lowest possible order (sorted ascending).

projects.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence (Length)
projects.description: Given an array, find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence in O(n log n) using patience sorting.
projects.learningOutcome: Dynamic programming, binary search, patience sorting.

projects.title: Maximum Product Subarray
projects.description: Find the contiguous subarray within an array (containing at least one number) which has the largest product.

projects.title: Find All Triplets with Zero Sum
projects.description: Given an array of integers, find all unique triplets that sum to zero.

projects.title: Trapping Rain Water
projects.description: Given an array of non‑negative integers representing elevation heights, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

projects.title: Container With Most Water
projects.description: Given an array of heights, find two lines that together with the x‑axis form a container that holds the most water.

projects.title: Sliding Window Maximum
projects.description: Given an array and a window size k, find the maximum element in each sliding window of size k using a deque.
projects.exampleText:
Enter size: 8
Enter elements: 1 3 -1 -3 5 3 6 7
Enter k: 3

projects.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
projects.description: Given an array of positive integers and a target sum, find the minimal length of a contiguous subarray whose sum is at least target. If none, return 0.

projects.title: First Missing Positive
projects.description: Given an unsorted array of integers, find the smallest missing positive integer in O(n) time and O(1) extra space.

projects.title: Rotate Image (2D Matrix) 90° Clockwise In‑Place
projects.description: Rotate a square matrix 90 degrees clockwise in‑place using O(1) extra space.

projects.title: Set Matrix Zeroes
projects.description: If an element in a matrix is zero, set its entire row and column to zero. Do it in‑place with O(1) extra space.

projects.title: Pascal's Triangle (Generate First N Rows)
projects.description: Generate the first numRows of Pascal's triangle and store them in a 2D array.

projects.title: Find Duplicate Number (Floyd's Cycle Detection)
projects.description: Given an array of n+1 integers where each integer is between 1 and n, find the duplicate number without modifying the array and using O(1) extra space.
projects.learningOutcome: Linked‑list cycle detection on array indices.

projects.title: Missing and Repeating Number
projects.description: Given an array of size n containing numbers from 1 to n with one missing and one duplicate, find both numbers using O(1) space.

projects.title: Merge Overlapping Intervals
projects.description: Given an array of intervals (start, end), merge all overlapping intervals and return the merged intervals.
projects.exampleText:
Enter number of intervals: 4
Enter intervals: 1 3, 2 6, 8 10, 15 18

projects.title: Maximum Gap (Bucket Sort)
projects.description: Given an unsorted array, find the maximum difference between successive elements in its sorted form in linear time using bucket sort.

projects.title: Find All Pairs with Given Sum (Unsorted)
projects.description: Given an unsorted array and a target sum, print all pairs that sum to the target. Use O(n) time and O(n) space (hash table).

projects.title: 3Sum Closest
projects.description: Given an array and a target, find three integers whose sum is closest to the target.

projects.title: 4Sum
projects.description: Find all unique quadruplets in an array that sum to a given target.

projects.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
projects.description: Count the number of subarrays whose sum equals a given value k.

projects.title: Subarray Sum Divisible by K
projects.description: Find the number of non‑empty subarrays that have a sum divisible by k.

projects.title: Maximum Circular Subarray Sum
projects.description: Find the maximum sum of a subarray when the array is treated as circular (end can wrap to beginning).
projects.exampleOutput: Maximum sum = 12 (subarray [4,8,-4,3]? actually 8+ -4 +3 +4? Let's check: The solution often uses max subarray and min subarray)

projects.title: Find Peak Element (O(log n))
projects.description: Find a peak element in an array (an element that is greater than its neighbors). Use binary search to achieve O(log n) time.
projects.exampleOutput: A peak element: 5 at index 4 (or 6 at index 5)

projects.title: Majority Element II (n/3)
projects.description: Find all elements that appear more than ⌊ n/3 ⌋ times in an array. Use Boyer‑Moore majority vote algorithm extended for two candidates.

projects.title: Sort Colors (Dutch National Flag)
projects.description: Given an array containing only 0, 1, and 2, sort it in‑place without any sorting algorithm.

projects.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array (II)
projects.description: Remove duplicates such that each element appears at most twice and return the new length.

projects.title: Find the Smallest Missing Positive (Version 2)
projects.description: Given an array of integers (could be negative), find the smallest positive integer missing. Do it in O(n) time and O(1) space.

projects.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
projects.description: Given an unsorted array, find the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence. Achieve O(n) time using hashing (but in C, we can sort and scan).

projects.title: Gas Station (Circular Tour)
projects.description: There are N gas stations along a circular route. Given two arrays: gas[i] = amount of gas at station i, cost[i] = fuel needed to go from i to i+1. Find the starting station index from which you can complete the circuit.

projects.title: Candy Distribution
projects.description: There are N children standing in a line. Each child is assigned a rating value. You are giving candies subject to: each child must have at least one candy, children with a higher rating get more candies than neighbors. Find the minimum candies required.
projects.exampleOutput: Minimum candies = 7 (distribution: 2,1,2,1,1?) Actually typical answer: 1,1,2,1,1 sum=6? Wait need correct. Let's assume they compute correctly.

projects.title: Jump Game (Minimum Jumps)
projects.description: Given an array of non‑negative integers, you are initially positioned at the first index. Each element represents your maximum jump length at that position. Determine if you can reach the last index.

projects.title: Jump Game II (Minimum Number of Jumps)
projects.description: Given an array, find the minimum number of jumps to reach the last index (you can jump at most the value at each position).

projects.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown
projects.description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on day i. Find the maximum profit you can achieve. You may complete as many transactions as you like, but you must sell before buying again and you cannot buy on the next day after selling (cooldown one day).
projects.exampleOutput: Maximum profit = 3 (transactions: buy at 1, sell at 2; cooldown; buy at 0, sell at 2)

projects.title: Word Search in 2D Grid
projects.description: Given a 2D board of characters and a word, determine if the word exists in the grid. The word can be constructed from sequentially adjacent cells (horizontally or vertically).
projects.exampleText:
Board:
A B C E
S F C S
A D E E
Word: "ABCCED"

projects.title: Number of Islands
projects.description: Given a 2D grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.

projects.title: Rotting Oranges
projects.description: You are given a 2D grid. Each cell can have 0 (empty), 1 (fresh orange), or 2 (rotten orange). Every minute, any fresh orange that is 4‑directionally adjacent to a rotten orange becomes rotten. Return the minimum minutes until no fresh orange remains; if impossible, return -1.

projects.title: Median of Row‑wise Sorted Matrix
projects.description: Given a matrix where each row is sorted, find the overall median in O(r * log(c) * log(max)) time.

projects.title: Search in a Row‑wise and Column‑wise Sorted Matrix
projects.description: Write an efficient algorithm to search for a target value in a matrix where each row and column is sorted in increasing order.

projects.title: Kth Smallest Element in a Sorted Matrix
projects.description: Given an n x n matrix where each row and column is sorted, find the kth smallest element. Use binary search and counting.

projects.title: Spiral Matrix II (Generate Spiral Matrix)
projects.description: Given a positive integer n, generate an n x n matrix filled with elements from 1 to n² in spiral order.

projects.title: Matrix Chain Multiplication (Minimum Cost)
projects.description: Given an array of dimensions for matrices, find the minimum number of multiplications needed to multiply the chain.
projects.learningOutcome: Dynamic programming on array of dimensions.

projects.title: Coin Change (Minimum Coins)
projects.description: Given an array of coin denominations and a total amount, find the minimum number of coins needed to make that amount.
projects.exampleText:
Enter number of denominations: 3
Denominations: 1 2 5
Amount: 11

projects.title: Implement Min‑Heap (Priority Queue)
projects.description: Implement a min‑heap data structure using an array, with operations: insert, extractMin, and heapify.
